title: mentha longifolia var. subintegra
intro: Mentha longifolia var. subintegra, also known as Horehound, is a subspecies of the Horehound plant, a member of the mint family with long, narrow leaves and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The medicinal properties of Horehound have been shown to provide numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating digestive issues. Horehound can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup for its medicinal properties, and is also used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation and ease respiratory discomfort.
